titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/120

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=397 status=live modified=2021-04-06 14:56:54.578593+00 | translation moa=398 status=live modified=2021-04-06 14:56:56.403713+00

p.span:
Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis tam presentibus quam futuris  Comes Patricius Salutem . Sciatis me
dedisse et hac mea carta confirmasse deo et ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros . et mona
chis ibidem deo seruientibus . pro animabus omnium antecessorum et successorum meorum . in liberam
et puram et perpetuam elemosinam . duas acras terre et dimidiam apud harecarres . scilicet
sicut fossatum circuit eandem terram . tenendas de me et heredibus meis . ita libere . quiete .
plenarie . et honorifice sicut ullam elemosinam liberius . quietius . plenius . et honorificen
tius in terra Regis tenent et possident . ab omni terreno seruitio . et consuetudine . et exac
tione seculari omnino liberas et quietas in perpetuum . His T . Rolland’ . de Rollan’ . Se
nescallo . Bernardo . fresel . Gilleberto . de Hom . Nez . Walt’ . Ketello . de letham .
Oliuero filio kylu . Patricio de fachenus . Gilleberto . clerico meo . Petro de Witingeh’ .

div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church as well present as future Earl Patrick sends greeting. Know me to have given and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, for the souls of all my ancestors and predecessors, in free and pure and perpetual alms, two and a half acres of land at Harcarse, that is, as the ditch encloses the same land, to be held of me and my heirs as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as they hold and possess any alms freely, quitely, fully, and honourably in the king's land, free and quit from every earthly service and custom and secular exaction for ever. With these witnesses: Roland de Roland, steward; Bernard Fraser; Gilbert of Home; Ness of Walton; Ketill of Letham; Oliver, son of Kilvert; Patrick de Fachener; Gilbert, my clerk; Peter of Whittinghame.
